business.headline: IndiGo shares hit 52-week low, fall nearly 6%: What is dragging the stock today?IndiGo shares tanked nearly 6% to Rs 3,899.90, down Rs 248 or 5.98%. During the session, it touched a 52-week low of Rs 3,894.80.

China eyes Iranian oil deals after US grants limited sanctions reliefThe US has given a 30-day waiver, allowing some Iranian oil to be sold in order to prevent a sharp jump in global prices. While the relief is temporary, it has opened a small window for buyers, especially in Asia.

Safe haven no more? Why gold, silver prices are crashing despite warGold and silver price are falling even as global tensions rise, turning the usual safe-haven play on its head and catching investors off guard.

HDFC Bank share price falls 3%, hits 52-week low: Why is the stock down?Shares of HDFC Bank were down 3.39% to Rs 754 on the Bombay Stock Exchange at around 10:23 am, extending a sharp decline seen over the past few sessions.

Sensex crashes 1,800 points: Why is the stock market falling today?As of 12:33 pm, the S&P BSE Sensex was down 1,866.05 points at 72,666.91, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 608.10 points to 22,506.40. Both indices were down around 2.5%, reflecting broad-based selling across sectors.

Rupee slides to record low, nears 94 as crude oil surge deepens pressureCrude prices have surged sharply in recent weeks as the conflict in West Asia intensifies. Prices are now hovering above $110 per barrel, a level that immediately hurts countries like India, which import over 80% of their oil needs.

Markets bleed as Hormuz remains on edge; Sensex down 1,800, Rupee at record lowThe Middle East conflict, now entering its fourth week, has intensified after fresh warnings around the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, raising concerns about potential supply disruptions and a prolonged energy shock.

Brent crude oil hovering above $110 a barrel. Why it spells bad newsMore than three weeks after US and Israeli strikes on Iran triggered a chain of retaliation across the region, the conflict has now spread across West Asia. What started as a geopolitical escalation is now turning into an economic one, and oil is at the centre of it.

HDFC Bank to be in focus today on Dalal Street. Here's all you need to knowThe stock has seen a steep decline recently. In just two sessions, the share price fell from around Rs 843 to Rs 781 on the NSE, marking a drop of over 7%. After closing at Rs 781 on Friday, the stock is now only Rs 11 above its 52-week low of Rs 770.

$53 billion wiped out: Middle East war shakes global airline industryAs the war enters its fourth week, airlines are struggling to cope with a sharp rise in jet fuel prices, which have surged significantly in a short span of time. The impact is already visible for passengers, with ticket prices expected to rise in the coming months as airlines try to protect already thin profit margins.

Oil shock deepens as West Asia war escalates. Is a global recession coming?The world is already feeling the heat as the West Asia conflict enters its fourth week, pushing global crude oil prices higher. In this article, we speak to experts to understand whether this shock could tip the world toward a recession.

Govt nod to hike commercial LPG allocation to 50% as domestic output improvesThe additional allocation will be subject to commercial establishments registering with oil companies and applying for a piped natural gas connection, according to a letter written by the oil secretary to states.

Flight tickets likely to get costlier from April 1. Here's whyCivil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu said that the impact of the ongoing Iran war on the aviation sector is likely to be visible from April 1, when the Air Turbine Fuel (ATF) prices will be revised.

Raymond's Chairman Gautam Singhania injured as his speedboat overturns in MaldivesGautam Singhania sustained minor injuries after a speedboat capsized in the Maldives. Two Indian nationals remain missing following the accident.

May lead to spike in fares: Airlines oppose 60% free seat ruleA new government directive on free seat allocation has triggered sharp opposition from airlines, who warn it could push up fares, hurt affordability and reshape how tickets are priced across India's aviation sector.

Why bank lockers are hard to get and what you can do insteadThis is the third part of Lock & Key, a series that looks at how India's bank locker system works, the risks involved, and what customers need to know. After examining safety and insurance in the first two parts, this story focusses on a new problem – access.

World economy in serious trouble, India vulnerable: Raghuram Rajan on oil crisisThe former RBI Govenor said a 15–20% global energy disruption would require sharp demand cuts, pushing oil to $150–$200 per barrel, risking severe economic stress if the conflict continues another month.

Oil could hit $150-$200 per barrel if Iran war drags on: Raghuram RajanFormer RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan warned that if the US-Israeli war against Iran continues beyond a month, oil prices could surge to $150-$200 per barrel, potentially triggering a global recession.

Premium petrol prices up by Rs 2, no change in normal petrol, diesel ratesIndian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have increased premium petrol prices by ₹2 per litre, with IOCL XP95 now priced at ₹101.80 per litre. The hike affects premium petrol used mostly in performance vehicles, while regular petrol prices remain unchanged.
Sensex ends 326 points higher, Nifty above 23,100; HDFC Bank falls 2%The S&P BSE Sensex added 325.72 points to end at 74,532.96, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 112.35 points to end at 23,114.50.

entertainment.headline: How Ranveer's Dhurandhar beat SRK's Jawan, Pathaan at box office in single weekend Dhurandhar: The Revenge is only a weekend old, and it has already shattered the biggest records set by the previous Hindi blockbusters like Jawan, Pathaan and Animal. Here's a detailed look at its records, and the comparison with other biggies.

Dhurandhar 2's Uzair, Danish Pandor, calls Ranveer one of the finest actors Danish Pandor, who plays Uzair Baloch in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, praises Ranveer Singh as one of the finest actors of this generation, highlighting his irreplaceable contribution to the film.

I'm still processing it: Arjun Rampal says he's humbled by Dhurandhar 2 response Arjun Rampal, who plays Major Iqbal in Dhurandhar 2, said he is still processing the response to the film. The actor credited the film's team for shaping his villainous role of ISI chief.

Weak detailing in Dhurandhar 2? Fans say they spot editing gaffes in Ranveer film Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues to dominate the box office while drawing attention for a few noticeable on-screen errors. Viral clips of these slip-ups have sparked conversations online.

Meet Mustafa Ahmed, Ranveer Singh's trainer who played spy Rizwan in Dhurandhar From gym floors to film sets, Mustafa Ahmed's resilience defines his unique path in Bollywood. He plays Rizwan in Ranveer Singh-starrer Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Dhurandhar my revenge for what I saw that night: Arjun Rampal recalls 26/11 horror Actor Arjun Rampal revealed that Dhurandhar was his way of channelising his angst and taking his "revenge" for everything that he had to endure during the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.

Smriti Irani clears air on Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi spin-off rumours Smriti Irani reacted as rumours of a Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi spin-off gained traction. The actor-turned-politician clarified that she is not associated with any show other than Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi.

Badshah's Tateeree row escalates: Haryana Police remove 857 links, intensify action Haryana Police has taken down 857 social media links linked to Badshah's controversial song Tateeree amid growing legal heat. The crackdown comes as authorities warn of strict action and the controversy deepens.

Dhurandhar 2 music: Baazigar to Tamma Tamma, love classics in the time of gore Dhurandhar: The Revenge uses popular old Bollywood songs and reworks them with modern EDM and hip-hop sounds to suit today's audience. By placing these tracks in action-heavy scenes, the film turns familiar melodies into storytelling tools.

Singer Chappell Roan apologises as footballer alleges mistreatment by her security Chappell Roan apologised in the hotel security dispute involving Brazilian footballer Jorginho's family during Lollapalooza Brazil. The singer said the security guard involved in the incident was not her personal security guard and that she didn't even see the incident.

Dhurandhar's Gaurav Gera digs up 1998 letter to parents on early struggling days Dhurandhar actor Gaurav Gera revisited a letter that he sent to his parents in 1998. The emotional note offered a glimpse into his journey before making it into films.

Kajal Aggarwal calls Ranveer Singh a legend after Dhurandhar 2; shares review Kajal Aggarwal called Ranveer Singh a 'legend of our times' while reviewing Dhurandhar The Revenge. The actor also praised Aditya Dhar's direction, the cast's performances, and the music as the film dominates the box office.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 4: Ranveer's film beats Gadar 2 worldwide, Chhaava next Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has wrapped up its first weekend with an impressive worldwide total. The spy thriller is now eyeing Chhaava and Stree 2, while also targeting entry into the Rs 1,000 crore club within its first week.

Alia Bhatt cheers for Dhurandhar 2, calls Ranveer Singh-Aditya Dhar magic in sync Alia Bhatt praised Ranveer Singh's performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, highlighting a powerful moment from the film.

The 50 finale: Shiv Thakare wins show, lucky fan takes home Rs 50 lakh prize money Shiv Thakare emerged as the winner of The 50, lifting the trophy, while his fan Sitaram Pralhad Aghav received the Rs 50 lakh prize.

Selfies, cheers and 'Babbar Sher': Ranveer, Deepika step out amid Dhurandhar 2 buzz Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh stepped out for lunch amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Now, selfies and a video of fans cheering the Bollywood couple, are going viral.

Get info from better sources: Vishal Dadlani on Dhurandhar 2 demonetisation track Vishal Dadlani seemingly took a dig at Dhurandhar: The Revenge over its depiction of demonetisation, sharing posts that questioned its real-world impact.

Me with the Power Couple: RGV's Dhurandhar photo-op with Aditya Dhar, Yami Gautam Ram Gopal Varma met Aditya Dhar and Yami Gautam, calling them a "power couple" while continuing to praise Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Dhurandhar 2: Watch Ranveer Singh's ghost transformation BTS clip Celebrity make-up artist Preetisheel Singh shared a BTS video revealing Ranveer Singh's intricate "ghost born from shadows" transformation in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

general news.headline: Pilots dead after plane rams truck at New York's LaGuardia airport after landingAccording to preliminary reports, the Air Canada plane collided with a fire truck while heading to a gate amid inclement weather conditions. Four firefighters were injured.

Dhurandhar my revenge for what I saw that night: Arjun Rampal recalls 26/11 horrorActor Arjun Rampal revealed that Dhurandhar was his way of channelising his angst and taking his "revenge" for everything that he had to endure during the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.

Safe haven no more? Why gold, silver prices are crashing despite warGold and silver price are falling even as global tensions rise, turning the usual safe-haven play on its head and catching investors off guard.

Stop Truck 1, stop! Audio captures ATC panic as Air Canada jet hits vehicle at NY airportA minute-long ATC recording lays bare the confusion and urgency before a runway collision in New York. Controllers issued frantic warnings as a plane struck a vehicle, forcing a complete airport shutdown.

The original Dhurandhar. Who was Ravindra Kaushik, RAW's Black Tiger?India's original Dhurandhar, Ravindra Kaushik, rose from acting at college theatres, to infiltrating the Pakistan Army as a RAW Agent. He provided critical intelligence on Pakistani troop movements and the country's nuclear programme, but died a lonely death after his betrayal and subsequent capture by the ISI.

Live: PM Modi to speak on West Asia crisis at 2 pm in Parliament todayBudget Session Live Updates: Proceedings in both Houses of Parliament resumed at 11 am today. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is set to introduce the Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill in the Lok Sabha to further amend the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008 and the Companies Act, 2013. Last week, Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla revoked the suspension of eight Opposition MPs, who had been barred for the remainder of the Budget Session over "unruly behaviour. On Wednesday, the Rajya Sabha bid farewell to 59 members from 20 states. Addressing the House, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said their contributions to parliamentary democracy would always be remembered. Following his remarks, Parliament was adjourned till Monday, March 23. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Live: Saudi intercepts air raid over eastern region; US hits Iran's drone plantUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is now in its fourth week and the stakes just keep getting higher. After US President Donald Trump on Saturday (IST) gave Iran a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran appeared defiant and said if his threat of attacking power plants materialised, Iranian forces would target critical infrastructure and energy facilities in the region. Iran further said that it would tighten its blockade of the strait if the US attacks its power grid. In another development, Iran claimed that it intercepted and targeted a US F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island after the warplane allegedly violated Iranian airspace. The US, however, denied Iran's claim, calling it a 'rumour.' On a second front, US ally Israel has opened an investigation to ascertain how Iranian missiles were able to evade its air defence systems during the strikes on southern Israel on Saturday (local time). Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 4: Ranveer's film beats Gadar 2 worldwide, Chhaava nextRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has wrapped up its first weekend with an impressive worldwide total. The spy thriller is now eyeing Chhaava and Stree 2, while also targeting entry into the Rs 1,000 crore club within its first week.

Meet Professor Jiang: The Chinese Nostradamus who doesn't talk about China Tucker Carlson says he might cry listening to him. Millions call him a prophet. But who exactly is "Professor Jiang", and what is he not telling you?

Sanjay Bangar's daughter Anaya undergoes gender-affirming surgery: What is it?Anaya Bangar, daughter of former cricketer Sanjay Bangar, recently underwent gender-affirming surgery in Thailand. This surgery refers to a set of medical procedures that helps a person align their physical characteristics with their gender identity.

PSL's loss is IPL's gain: How many players have quit Pakistan league so far?The PSL's overlap with the IPL over the last two seasons has created a growing problem for the PCB, with players increasingly leaving the Pakistan Super League for lucrative IPL replacement deals.

Iran charging ships $2 million for passage through Strait of Hormuz: ReportThe Strait of Hormuz is a critical choke point for global fuel transit and has remained effectively closed since the first week of March in the wake of the ongoing war. About 20 per cent of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) is transported through the critical shipping lane.

IIT team uncovers 4.4-billion-year-old mystery about the MoonThe study focuses on a rare class of iron- and titanium-rich rocks known as ilmenite-bearing cumulates (IBC), believed to have formed around 4.3-4.4 billion years ago when a vast ocean of molten rock covered the Moon.

Petrol Shortage League: Fans troll PSL over oil crisis stepsPakistan Super League is being trolled by cricket fans after the league decided to trim down its operations. The reaction comes after PCB head Mohsin Naqvi announced that the league will held in only 2 cities this season. That too, behind closed doors.

I was offered lavender marriage. A closer look revealed the dark realityI was proposed lavender marriage twice within five months. Such moments seemingly unfold daily on matrimonial apps as queer people turn to this arrangement for social camouflage. A woman is offered a life of opulence abroad, but the emotional cost is huge and legal guardrails near-zero.

War and weather collide: Middle East staring at unprecedented rainsSevere weather will add to the challenges amid ongoing conflict in the region, threatening severe economic consequences and possible death toll.

Rally icon, sailing legend missing after boat crash that left Gautam Singhania hurtA high-speed boat crash in the Maldives has left two Indians missing, including a celebrated rally figure, even as Gautam Singhania returns home. Search teams continue efforts amid mounting concern and unanswered questions.
The death of 'lived-in homes' in IndiaIn the race to design the "perfect" home, are we losing what made homes feel like ours?

How Saudi Arabia came to control Mecca, MedinaThe Middle East is a raging inferno with Saudi Arabia warning Iran of retaliation after it hit Riyadh. Shia powerhouse Iran had questioned the legitimacy of Saudi Arabia's Al Saud family as custodians of Mecca and Medina. This is how Saudi Arabia, born in 1932, got to control Islam's two holiest sites, which are centuries-old.

China eyes Iranian oil deals after US grants limited sanctions reliefThe US has given a 30-day waiver, allowing some Iranian oil to be sold in order to prevent a sharp jump in global prices. While the relief is temporary, it has opened a small window for buyers, especially in Asia.

Gaganyaan astronauts channel inner Superman, train in vomit cometThe Zero-G exercises are part of a broader training programme led by the Indian Space Research Organisation, aimed at ensuring astronauts are mission-ready.

Markets bleed as Hormuz remains on edge; Sensex down 1,800, Rupee at record lowThe Middle East conflict, now entering its fourth week, has intensified after fresh warnings around the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, raising concerns about potential supply disruptions and a prolonged energy shock.

Satellite captures massive cloud cover shrouding Delhi-NCR. See picsThe weather patterns suggest that scattered light to moderate rain may persist in parts of Delhi-NCR and adjoining areas over the next two to three days.

Live: BSEB Inter results via Press Conference at 1:30 pmBSEB Class 12 Result 2026 Live: The Bihar School Examination Board is set to declare the Class 12 (Intermediate) results today, bringing an end to the long wait for lakhs of students across Science, Commerce, and Arts streams. Candidates are eagerly awaiting their scores as the results play a key role in shaping their higher education and career paths. Stay tuned here for live updates, direct result links, pass percentage details, topper announcements, and other important highlights.

sports.headline: New Zealand Cricket director resigns after NZ20 replaces iconic Super Smash New Zealand Cricket has backed a new NZ20 franchise league to replace the Super Smash. Hours after the decision was made official, board director Dion Nash issued his resignation.

Virat Kohli hits rapid 29, Tim David, Venkatesh Iyer bat well in RCB practice Royal Challengers Bengaluru held their first practice game on Sunday, March 22, ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Their batters came off the blocks quickly and batted with aggressive intent.

Petrol Shortage League: Fans troll PSL over oil crisis steps Pakistan Super League is being trolled by cricket fans after the league decided to trim down its operations. The reaction comes after PCB head Mohsin Naqvi announced that the league will held in only 2 cities this season. That too, behind closed doors.
Messi scores 901st career goal as Inter Miami rally past New York Lionel Messi scored his 901st career goal as Inter Miami came from behind to beat New York City FC 3-2 in a thrilling MLS clash.

Real Madrid beat Atletico in pulsating derby, stay alive in LaLiga race Real Madrid vs Atletico Madrid: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Real Madrid came from behind to beat Atletico Madrid 3-2 in a thrilling derby, keeping their LaLiga title push firmly on track.

Carlos Alcaraz stunned by world No. 36 Sebastian Korda in Miami Open Miami Open: World no.1 Carlos Alcaraz has been stunned by United States Sebastian Korda in the third round of the Miami Open. Korda, ranked 36th in the world, bested Alcaraz in three sets.

Hardik Pandya's MI goals in 2026: Win 6th title, build legacy Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya wants to win the 6th Indian Premier League title for the franchise in IPL 2026. In an interview, Hardik said that it was important to him that MI played their best-ever cricket this season.

PSL's loss is IPL's gain: How many players have quit Pakistan league so far? The PSL's overlap with the IPL over the last two seasons has created a growing problem for the PCB, with players increasingly leaving the Pakistan Super League for lucrative IPL replacement deals.

Arsenal's ship springs first hole as Man City snatch Carabao Cup from Arteta Arsenal's push for a historic season hit its first roadblock as Manchester City punished their mistakes in the Carabao Cup final. With bigger prizes still in sight, Arteta's side must respond quickly to avoid this becoming a familiar pattern again.

How World Cup win changed women's cricket in India: Jemimah Rodrigues explains In an interview with Sports Today, Jemimah Rodrigues said India's 2025 Women's World Cup win has inspired a new generation of young cricketers and boosted recognition for female players.

Suresh Raina, Matthew Hayden inducted into CSK's first-ever Hall of Fame IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have inducted legends Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden into their first-ever Hall of Fame, honouring their outstanding contributions to the franchise.

Virat Kohli reminisces RCB's deserving IPL 2025 triumph: It was not a flukeIPL 2026: Virat Kohli said RCB's IPL 2025 title win was no fluke. He highlighted the team's confidence and consistency throughout the campaign.

MS Dhoni asked to play till 60, chat with actor Sivakarthikeyan goes viralIPL 2026: MS Dhoni joked about playing IPL till he is 60-year-old during a CSK fan event. His response to actor Sivakarthikeyan sparked fresh excitement among fans.

PCB will take action against players opting out of PSL for IPL: Mohsin NaqviPCB chief Mohsin Naqvi has warned that players who leave the PSL for the IPL will face action under league rules, citing Corbin Bosch's one-year ban last year as a precedent.

Watch: Suresh Raina rolls back years, hits Noor Ahmed for six in front of MS DhoniIPL 2026: Suresh Raina thrilled Chepauk with a vintage six off Noor Ahmed. The moment, with MS Dhoni watching on, sent fans into nostalgia mode.

Rishabh Pant with most pressure in IPL 2026: Du Plessis urges LSG captain to deliverIPL 2026: Faf du Plessis believes Rishabh Pant will face the most pressure in IPL 2026. The LSG captain will need to deliver after a disappointing 2025 season.

MS Dhoni steals spotlight at CSK reunion, honours Sanju Samson, Shivam DubeMS Dhoni stole the spotlight at CSK's 'Roar 26' event in Chennai, as former stars, including Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden, reunited at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Sunday. The former captain also honoured World Cup-winning stars Sanju Samson, Shivam Dube and Ayush Mhatre ahead of IPL 2026.

Thatrom Thookrom: Sanju Samson thrills CSK fans with Tamil punchlineIPL 2026: Sanju Samson thrilled CSK fans with a Tamil punchline at the ROAR 2026 event. His words and presence sparked massive cheers at Chepauk.

Australia Women shrug off India setback to hammer West Indies in T20I seriesAUSW vs WIW, 2nd T20I: Australia Women sealed the T20I series against West Indies with a 17-run win in the second match in Kingstown, bouncing back strongly after last month's 2-1 home series defeat to India and taking an unassailable 2-0 lead.

PSL continues to lose players as Shanaka set to join IPL as injury replacementIPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka is set to leave PSL side Lahore Qalandars to join Rajasthan Royals as Sam Curran's replacement. His move adds to the growing list of players choosing IPL over PSL.

KKR fans furious as bowling coach Bravo spotted at CSK event during injury crisisIPL 2026: KKR fans erupted in anger after bowling coach Dwayne Bravo was spotted at CSK's event, raising questions about divided loyalties amid a mounting injury crisis at the franchise.

South Africa hold back New Zealand in Wellington to force T20I series deciderSouth Africa beat New Zealand by 19 runs in the fourth T20I in Wellington to level the five-match series at 2-2 and force a decider, after posting 164 for five and bowling the hosts out for 145 in 18.5 overs.

Was Rishabh Pant leaving LSG? Sanjiv Goenka breaks silence, laughs off rumoursIPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants owner Sanjiv Goenka, speaking to India Today's sister channel SportsTak, backed Rishabh Pant to deliver and admitted the team's mistakes last season cost them a top-four finish.

Shreyanka Patil: Inspired by Rahul Dravid's Adelaide 233 and Virat Kohli, I made my comebackShreyanka Patil, in an exclusive interview with India Today Digital, said that Rahul Dravid's inspirational 233 in Adelaide and Virat Kohli's remarkable innings at the venue inspired her comeback.

PSL opening ceremony cancelled, games behind closed doorsPSL: The Pakistan Cricket Board has cancelled the PSL 2026 opening ceremony as fuel shortage started showing its effects on the country. PSL will go ahead as scheduled but with major changes, revealed Mohsin Naqvi.

Virat Kohli laughs off claims he sought charter flights to London for IPL 2026IPL 2026: Virat Kohli laughed off claims he sought charter flights to London during IPL 2026, responding to social media rumours ahead of Royal Challengers Bengaluru's season opener in Bengaluru.

Lucknow Super Giants make big call to help accommodate Rishabh Pant in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant is expected to be LSG's new No.3 while Nicholas Pooran gets demoted to the middle-order despite being one of their standout players last season.

Pakistan fined for poor T20 WC 2026: Kirsten says he isn't shocked about newsGary Kirsten said that he was sad to hear the news that the Pakistan players were being fined for their performance in the T20 World Cup 2026. Kirtsen, however, admitted that he wasn't surprised to hear about the move from PCB.

Gavaskar stands his ground, calls for IPL-owned teams to stop paying Pak playersSunil Gavaskar has called on Indian-owned franchises to stop paying Pakistani players, while hitting back at accusations of hypocrisy following his controversial column on the matter.

Premier League title race on hold as Manchester City, Arsenal eye Carabao Cup winWith the Premier League title race put on hold for the moment, Arsenal and Manchester City will meet in the final of the Carabao Cup 2025-26. With silverware up for grabs, which team willk seize the chance to gain the edge and the title.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
